summary: Tested multi-class feature end-to-end (backend + frontend). Backend: 10/10 new multi-class tests PASS (classes CRUD, admin auth, class_id filtering on children & overview, cascade delete, last-class guard, backfill). Old test_api.py also updated to include class_id; 8/10 pass (2 failures are pre-existing assertions on stale seed data - children/workshops seeds were customized by previous iterations, not a multi-class regression). Frontend: full happy-path PASSED - ClassPicker auto-redirect with 1 class, multi-class picker grid, HomePage shows class emoji + name with back-to-classes button, Admin Classes tab (add + delete + cascade), Admin Enfants class selector scopes listing + creation, Admin Suivi class selector scopes overview rows, workshops remain global (same columns in overview regardless of class). E2E: created 'TEST_PS_UI' class + 'TEST_Kid_UI' child, verified child appears ONLY on /classe/{ps_id} (home + overview) and NOT in the default class, then deleted class which cascaded the child. ChildWorkshopsPage back button returns to /classe/{child.class_id}.

minor.issue: Missing class_id returns 422 (pydantic) rather than a friendly 400 error. Review contract (invalid class_id returns 400 'Classe invalide' which is good, but missing field returns raw pydantic 422 schema).

integration_issues.issue: On a fast click, home-title briefly shows fallback 'Mes ateliers' before klass state loads (race). Not user-facing critical but worth guarding with a loading state or waiting for klass before rendering title.

issues: Uses native browser <select> instead of shadcn Select component. Consider upgrading for consistent styling (matches remaining UI which already uses shadcn).

action_items: (Optional) Add loading skeleton to HomePage header instead of fallback text to avoid transient 'Mes ateliers' flash before klass loads.
(Optional) Return a user-friendly 400 when class_id is missing from POST /api/children (currently FastAPI returns 422 due to pydantic).
(Optional cleanup) Replace native <select> with shadcn Select for visual consistency with the rest of the admin UI.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminPage.jsx is 612 lines - consider splitting Classes/Children/Workshops/Overview tabs into separate files for maintainability.
HomePage.jsx: the Promise.all().catch(() => {}) swallows errors silently; no error toast if a class fetch fails (user sees blank/fallback UI).
server.py on_startup: backfilling children with the FIRST class by created_at is reasonable, but if operators rename 'Classe par défaut' the label no longer reflects reality. Not a bug, just a note.
Cascade delete on /classes/{id} correctly removes children and their validations - verified.
Workshops are intentionally global (no class_id) as per product spec - implementation matches.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_api.py (updated test_create_child_unauthorized and test_child_full_crud_and_validation_flow to pass class_id, required after schema change)

success_rate.backend: 100% (10/10 multi-class tests; 2 legacy seed-name tests unrelated)

seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_PS_UI class + TEST_Kid_UI child then delete (cascade). State restored to 1 class 'Classe par défaut'. No lingering TEST_ data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Env has 1 class ('Classe par défaut') + ~21 children (20 legacy + original seeds) + 2 workshops ('Scissor Practice', 'Easter Memory'). Default class_id changes per env. Use GET /api/classes to fetch it dynamically. The cannot-delete-last-class guard is covered but only actually exercised when count==1 (test skips destructive path otherwise). When only 1 class exists, '/' auto-redirects to /classe/{id}, so testing the picker requires adding a 2nd class first.
rca of the issue: No critical bugs found. Minor UX polish items only (see action_items).
